new_classes.label_en: PDO Still Wine in Containers of 2 Litres or Less with Low Actual Alcoholic Strength (CN 22042178)
new_classes.label_de: g.U.-Stillwein in Behältnissen bis 2 Liter mit niedrigem tatsächlichen Alkoholgehalt (KN 22042178)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes bearing a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO), presented in containers of 2 litres or less, with an actual alcoholic strength of more than 13 % vol but not exceeding 15 % vol, classified under CN subheading 2204 21 78 (Chapter 22). Distinguished from 22042174 (> 15 % vol) and other PDO still wine subheadings by alcoholic strength range.

coverage_explanation: The core distinguishing criteria for CN 22042178 — PDO still wine, container ≤ 2 L, actual alcoholic strength > 13 % vol and ≤ 15 % vol — are captured by the new class with its subclass relationship to PDOStillWineSmallContainer, PDO quality restriction, container volume range, and alcohol-by-volume range. The legal note references Additional Notes 4 and 5 to Chapter 22, which define 'total dry matter' (gesamter Trockenstoff) via non-volatile substances (sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colourants, salts). These compositional criteria for total dry matter determination are informational/interpretive notes rather than direct classification discriminators for this specific 8-digit code vs. its siblings (they apply equally across the full 22042111–22042198 range). No OWL property for total dry matter or individual non-volatile substance content is present in the TBox; introducing them here without the broader framework would be premature. Therefore coverage is partial: the primary classification discriminators (PDO, container size, alcoholic strength band) are fully covered; the total dry matter/non-volatile substance compositional detail is not formalised.
